title: The Scheduled Revelation
description: Orrery runs a 'precision futures consultation' from a rented clockwork booth in Brasston, with a seven-second mandatory delay on all appointments because the schedule requires it. She has been trying to reach Brigadier Flux for three weeks. Their appointment windows never align. She suspects this is not an accident.

offer.speaker: Madame Orrery
offer.text: Punctual. Good. Sit — the booth requires a seven-second delay before consultations begin. I don't make the rules. I've read them, which is worse.

offer.speaker: Madame Orrery
offer.text: I have a letter for Brigadier Flux. Three weeks I've tried to deliver it myself. His schedule and mine are offset by exactly eleven minutes — long enough that we never share an available slot. Exactly eleven. Every day. You see the problem.

offer.speaker: Madame Orrery
offer.text: Schedules drift. That's what schedules do. Ours don't drift — ours hold at eleven, precisely, like something is maintaining the gap. I'd like you to carry the letter across it.

accept.speaker: Madame Orrery
accept.text: Sealed. Don't read it — not for secrecy, for sequence. Some information arrives broken if it arrives out of order.

decline.speaker: Madame Orrery
decline.text: Then the letter waits. It's waited three weeks. The gap doesn't mind. That's what worries me about the gap.

in_progress.speaker: Madame Orrery
in_progress.text: Flux writes slowly and thinks slower, which is the correct order. Give him the time. Unfinished answers are how this world ended up like this.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Madame Orrery
ready_to_turn_in.text: His seal. Unbroken. Good — sit. The booth's seven seconds will feel long for this one. It should.

complete.speaker: Madame Orrery
complete.text: The same handwriting. On the Grand Mechanism blueprints — and in the Harmonia cave maintenance logs, dated forty years earlier. He's been sitting on this for decades, not knowing what it meant.

complete.speaker: Madame Orrery
complete.text: Cross-referenced with the symbol from your Phil's paper, it means this: whoever designed these worlds designed them all at once. The worlds aren't sequential. They were written simultaneously.

complete.speaker: Madame Orrery
complete.text: You're playing them in sequence. The author isn't.

locked.speaker: Madame Orrery
locked.text: The schedule says you're early. Not MY schedule — yours. There's a reading you haven't sat for yet.

objectives.description: Take Orrery's sealed letter across the eleven-minute gap.
Deliver the letter to Brigadier Flux and wait out his answer.
Bring Flux's response back to Orrery.

unlocks_dialogue.note: W4 chain step: windowless office, 'EXTERNAL CONSULTANT — APPROVED VENDOR #4429-C', the only external consultant never reassigned to internal training.

cross_world_connection: Chain A step 3 of 6. The world3_orrery cutscene (fired at turn-in) advances fool_card_marks to 3 and grants the cogsworth_calibration_key — which opens the pre-factory tunnels in W4 through a door that otherwise has no apparent function. The simultaneous-authorship revelation is the chain's midpoint thesis: the player experiences sequence; the Calibrant wrote all six at once.
_wiring_notes: DEDUP RULE: all grants (cogsworth_calibration_key) + fool_card_marks=3 live in the world3_orrery cutscene; quest carries exp only — validated shape per the enforced seam. Step 2 flavor beat (design doc): Flux's response takes seven minutes to write — waitable, or skippable; skipping hands the party an UNFINISHED DRAFT with different content. v1: single response_obtained flag either way; if the wait/skip fork is cheap to wire (timer interact vs walk-away re-interact), set quest_world3_scheduled_revelation_draft_unfinished alongside, and Orrery's turn-in gains one line acknowledging the incompleteness. SABOTEUR/NINJA VARIANT (design doc): a Saboteur-lead recognizes the eleven-minute offset as an artificially inserted scheduling constraint and can find the scheduling manifesto in the clockwork city's administrative gearwork — wire as a both-flags emitter (accepted + response_obtained at once, fine_print rogue-shortcut pattern) plus quest_world3_scheduled_revelation_manifesto_found for bonus lore dialogue: how the Calibrant maintains control through subtle inconvenience. The eleven-minute offset itself is the tell — same authored-friction fingerprint as Sprocket's one-tooth gear (world3_delay_in_everything); players who do both quests get the pattern without either quest naming it. Ordering fix 2026-07-09 (cowir-main): prereq_flag world2_orrery_complete enforces the sequential chain — steps 2-5 were orderless, letting a skip-ahead player hard-set marks out of order (early-armed finale / regressed counter).
